1.14: The firm may not act if the seller is a member of the conveyancer's immediate family.
1.15: The firm may not act if the borrower is a member of the conveyancer's immediate family.
3.1.3: Lender requires notification of the name and address of the seller's legal representative.
3.1.4: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if the seller does not have legal representation.
3.1.5: No documents other than standard identification are acceptable.
3.2.3: Lender requires notification of the name and address of the seller's legal representative.
3.2.4: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if the seller does not have legal representation.
4.5a: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if re-inspection is required.
4.5b: Certificate of title must be sent to the Lending Team at the address in section 1.11a.
4.1: Valuation report is provided electronically where possible.
Reports are not provided if an automated valuation model (AVM) was used.
4.3: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if valuation assumptions are incorrect.
5.1.1: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
5.2.1: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if the seller is not the registered owner.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are only required if specified in the mortgage offer or if they indicate a risk to security.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted if the firm subscribes to the Search Code (PCCB regulated).
Conveyancer must provide an unqualified Certificate of Title.
5.4.6: Local Authority Search Indemnity is permitted for remortgages only.
Solicitors obtain indemnity at own risk and must certify the title is good and marketable.
5.5.3a: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if breach of conditions will not be satisfied by completion.
5.5.3b: Planning permission documents should be placed with the Deeds; original/copy not required by lender.
5.5.3c: Building regulation consents should be placed with the Deeds; original/copy not required by lender.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development should be placed with the Deeds; original/copy not required by lender.
5.5.4: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if property restrictions affect value or marketability.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable up to 10% of the total property floor area.
5.7.1c: Report flying freehold titles to the Lending Team as per section 1.11a.
5.8.1: Converted buildings (max 4 flats) where the borrower occupies one are accepted if held as a share of freehold.
Lending is prohibited if the applicant is a leaseholder and the majority owner of the freehold.
5.8.5: Cross-freehold arrangements for two leasehold flats are not accepted.
5.10.1: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a regarding restrictions on use.

5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term: Mortgage term plus 50 years for both residential and buy-to-let.

5.14.10: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a for service charge matters.
5.14.11: For indemnity insurance regarding unsatisfactory lease terms, contact Settlements Team as per section 1.11a.

5.14.13: Notice of service evidence should be kept on file; do not send to lender.

5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association must be placed with the Deeds.

5.20.1: Solar panel leases must align with UK Finance Requirements.
Lease must include a break clause for mortgagee in possession.
5.20.3: Solar panel lease agreements must include a break clause for mortgagee in possession.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.

6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: NHBC, Zurich Municipal, BLP, LABC, Castle 10 (Checkmate), Build Assure, ICW, Aedis Warranties & The Q Policy, Build Zone, Premier Guarantee, One Guarantee, Global Home Warranties, Ark Residential, Advantage, Protek.
6.7.2: New home warranty documentation should be placed with the Deeds.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes should be kept on file.
6.7.4: Properties without a new home warranty scheme are not accepted.

8.1: The firm is not permitted to advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies should be kept on file.
9.2: Indemnity limit must be at least equal to the total mortgage advance.
Policy must protect borrowers, successors in title, and the mortgagee.
10.2b: Minimum notice for mortgage advance: Five business days.
10.3: Standard deductions are listed in the Mortgage Offer.
10.7: On delayed completion, advance must be returned by CHAPS the next business day.
10.9: Contact Lending Team as per section 1.11a if completion is delayed.
10.10: Funds can be held until the next business day after the original issue date.
10.11: Interest on delayed return of funds is charged at the borrower's mortgage rate.

If registration is not complete within 30 days, provide updates via LMS STARS every 30 days.
